steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Mudstone dominates the sediment, but shows episodic desiccation cracks — indicating a prolonged water environment that periodically dried, not a continuous one.

steps.title: Apply the signal to choices
steps.content:
This points to a lake (low-flow mudstone) that occasionally dried (cracks) and received stream input (the sandstone).

Choice B uniquely describes this: a prolonged lake with occasional drying and periodic stream presence.

The others either deny a lake, limit it to brief existence, or deny the drying.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
